response.justification: Wei Chen's career is overwhelmingly built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web, mobile, and embedded systems. Professional C++ experience is limited to approximately 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — far below the mandatory 5–10 years required. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, no SQL Server or Oracle usage, and no capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge; the closest financial exposure is a Node.js role at a retail investment startup (RIDE Capital) unrelated to capital markets systems.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, performance databases)
Strong software craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — applicable to any engineering environment
Financial-adjacent exposure at RIDE Capital, showing some familiarity with investment-domain product development
concerns: C++ experience is effectively negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) versus the mandatory 5–10 years
No capital markets, fixed income, or derivatives domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No SQL Server or Oracle experience mentioned; primary database background is MongoDB and MySQL, not enterprise financial-grade RDBMS platforms

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
